subject: Dispatch assertions: given (query, candidate-skills) tuples, verify the router selects the expected winner skill by applying the Routing Rules priority chain, deployment_target tiebreaker, legacy type fallback, and Fallback behavior.
schema_note: This is a v1 routing_eval artifact. Each case is a (query, candidates, expected_winner, reason) tuple. A consuming grader SHOULD implement the router's priority chain and assert that the winner matches expected_winner for each case. The format is deliberate: comprehension evals (examples/evals/skill-router.json) ask the model to explain what the router would do; routing evals (this file) ask a router implementation to actually do it. A skill declaring `routing_eval: present` ships one of these.

routing_cases.description: Surface 1 unique match — the trigger label resolves the dispatch without consulting surfaces 2 or 3.
routing_cases.reason: a11y is the only skill whose triggers array contains 'a11y-skill'. Priority 1 produces a unique winner — the chain stops.

routing_cases.description: Surface 2 unique match — no trigger is declared, path glob resolves the dispatch.
routing_cases.reason: a11y declares `paths: **/*.{html,tsx,jsx,vue,svelte}` which matches Modal.tsx. No other candidate's paths match. Priority 1 had no match, Priority 2 produces a unique winner.

routing_cases.description: Surface 3 unique match — keyword overlap with exactly one skill's keywords array.
routing_cases.reason: debugging's keywords include 'reproduce' and closely-related concepts; a11y and documentation do not share these keywords. Priority 3 produces a unique winner after priorities 1 and 2 had no match.

routing_cases.description: Three surfaces produce THREE different winners. The priority chain must stop at surface 1 and NOT consult surfaces 2 or 3. Mirrors comprehension eval #11.
routing_cases.reason: debugging-skill is a unique trigger match for debugging. Even though Modal.tsx would path-match a11y AND 'decide what to test' would keyword-match testing-strategy, the chain stops at Priority 1. A router that aggregated votes across surfaces here would be wrong.

routing_cases.description: Two skills tie on keyword score. Deployment-target tiebreaker breaks the tie in favor of `project` over `portable` when the project-specific skill is otherwise a fit. Mirrors comprehension eval #4.
routing_cases.reason: Both skills score similarly on keyword match. graph-audit is `deployment_target: project`; documentation is `deployment_target: portable`. When both remain valid candidates for a Skill Graph request, the project-specific deployment target wins over the portable one.

routing_cases.description: Two legacy manifest entries tie on keyword and deployment target. Legacy type fallback breaks the tie: `workflow` > `capability` > `router` > `overlay`. Mirrors comprehension eval #8.
routing_cases.reason: Both legacy entries are `deployment_target: portable`. debugging is `type: workflow`; a11y is `type: capability`. The legacy type fallback keeps older specimens deterministic, but current v8 skill metadata should not author new `type` claims.

routing_cases.description: Zero surfaces match. The router returns an explicit coverage-gap signal — NOT a nearest-match fallback and NOT a silent null. Mirrors comprehension eval #12.

expected_signal.message_contract: MUST surface an explicit coverage-gap signal to the caller indicating no skill matched. MUST recommend authoring a new skill or broadening an existing skill's keywords. MUST NOT fall back to a nearest-match skill. MUST NOT return null silently without the coverage-gap annotation.

routing_cases.reason: No skill in the library has triggers, paths, or keywords that match a translation-into-German query. The Fallback behavior doctrine is non-negotiable: surface the gap, do not guess. A router that returned 'documentation' here because German is prose-ish would be wrong.
